game_name: Might and Magic VII: For Blood and Honor

danger_signals.visual_form: Guards change stance/become aggressive toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Player has chosen opposing alignment path; entering wrong faction territory signals combat danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen display color shift (white for Light path, black for Dark path)
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent visual indicator of player's moral/political stance affecting world perception

danger_signals.visual_form: The Pit fortress in Deyja region
danger_signals.meaning: Visual marker of dangerous dark magic faction territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly NPCs and non-hostile guards in towns matching player's chosen path
safety_signals.meaning: City is safe haven for party; no combat threat from locals
Owned territory provides safe haven and base of operations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Elf characters appear as visual markers of faction territory; visual distinction from human soldiers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Elven lands of AvLee and Tularean Forest; natural forest environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Human soldiers and guards visually distinct; behavior changes based on player alignment choice
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Kingdom of Erathia; Harmondale (contested neutral realm); human architectural style

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goblin war parties break away from initial combat based on player party appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spawn near coast; implied goblin territories

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: The Pit stronghold visual design; necromancer-specific enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: The Pit fortress in Deyja barrens; dark magic environmental cues

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dwarf character race option indicates faction; appears in allied territories
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Barrow Downs (dwarven lands); underground/mountain architectural style

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen display becomes white-tinted; Light-aligned town becomes visually friendly
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards in Darkness-aligned towns become hostile; Light-aligned promotions become available; Light-specific quests unlock

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen display becomes black-tinted; Dark-aligned city (The Pit region) becomes visually friendly
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards in Light-aligned towns become hostile; Dark-aligned promotions become available; Dark-specific quests unlock

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Political tension between elves and humans
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Harmondale appears as contested borderland region; visual presence of both elf and human NPCs in conflict zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional timed quest to determine fate of realm; mandatory alignment choice with permanent story consequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Antagarich divided into regional territories with distinct visual styles; faction strongholds visible on world map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Heightened regional faction tensions; increased guard presence in disputed areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Characters emerge from sea in wetsuits; distinct appearance triggers visual recognition from elf and goblin war parties
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Immediate faction reactions—both sides cease fighting to observe the 'sea monsters'; establishes player party as external force

environmental_storytelling.example: AvLee and Tularean Forest visually identify as elven lands; Erathia as human kingdom; Barrow Downs as dwarven territory; The Pit as necromancer stronghold
Swamps and snow-capped mountains of Tatalia; Bracada desert; cloud city of Celeste; island isolation of Emerald Isle and Evernmorn communicate regional identity and accessibility

environmental_storytelling.technique: Castle ownership as visual reward/safe space
environmental_storytelling.example: Party's scavenger hunt-won castle serves as visual anchor of player achievement and operational base
NPC guard reactions shift from hostile to friendly based on alignment path, communicating political landscape changes without dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Non-hostile; allow free movement; protect town safety
Become aggressive/hostile; initiate combat on sight; block access to certain areas
Both sides break combat engagement to observe 'sea monsters'; recognize party as external threat/force
Friendly dialogue; quest availability; support party objectives

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Dark path chosen but in Light-aligned town
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile or dismissive; refuse quests; block progression

notes: Might and Magic VII uses alignment path (Light/Dark) as primary world-state manifestation mechanic. Visual communication is primarily through screen color tinting (white/black), NPC behavior shifts (guard hostility), and geographic faction identity. The game's political situation is communicated through territory control, quest availability, and alignment-locked promotions rather than explicit HUD indicators. The inheritance from Heroes of Might and Magic III and Might and Magic III creates a legacy-informed world where character arrival and faction positions are pre-established in lore.
